title: columnar val storage + undo journal (was jssm#756)
manager: Re-lay the machine's val storage in columnar form and back mutation with an undo-log journal — the performance keystone that makes extended state cheap to read in bulk and cheap to roll back when a contract fails. It is a big enabling-infra lever the later contract and survival eras lean on, so it ships gated behind a written perf envelope: the allowed regression budget is fixed and policed before the rewrite lands, because this is exactly the kind of change that silently costs 20% if nobody is watching.
cs: columnar val storage + undo journal (§28); write the runtime perf envelope (omissions D4) against the 6.0 graviton baseline FIRST, wired to the perf-envelope workflow so a breach blocks until justified.
